comment.name: Comment
comment.text:
Afternoon Report


Last Updated: 3/16/26 | 2:07PM

Good Afternoon, Sunday River!

The rain jackets were definitely the right call today. It's been a wet one, but the snow has been skiing great! Soft turns, smooth cruising, and there's still plenty of daylight left if you're thinking about getting in a few more afternoon laps. We may see a bit of wind later on, so keep an eye on the report for the most up to date info as the day rolls on. 

Looking at the rest of the week, the rain tapers off later tonight and we'll be back at it tomorrow for another day of turns. The rest of the week is trending in the right direction too, with sunshine and more classic winter conditions making their way back into the forecast. With the strong base we've built this season, the mountain is in great shape and ready to keep the good turns coming. 

Don't forget, the 2026/27 New England Pass is officially on sale and waiting for you. Renew now to lock in the lowest price of the year, and if you didn't have a pass this season, you can still grab one and ski the rest of this winter, too. Future you, and your friends already planning next winter's laps, will be glad you did.

We'll see you out there!

-Team Snow

comment.name: Comment 2
comment.text:
Good Morning, Sunday River!

Grab the Gore-Tex. Rain jackets might be the move today, but there's still plenty of fun to be had out there. Soft snow, smooth turns, and lots of mountain to explore are still on the menu. If you've got the right gear, it's a great day to get out there. We may also see some wind here and there today, so be sure to keep checking the report for the most up-to-date info as you plan your laps.

The grooming crew was busy overnight putting fresh corduroy across the usual favorites. Lifts start spinning at 9AM, and with temps climbing to around 40°, it's shaping up to be a pretty comfortable day out there. Some rain may be in the mix, but the milder air is a nice change of pace.

Looking ahead, the rest of the week is trending in the right direction. We're expecting a return to sunshine and more classic winter conditions. And with the strong base we've built this season, the mountain is in great shape and ready to keep the good turns rolling.

Already dreaming about next winter? It's officially time to get your 2026/27 New England Pass. Renew now to lock in the lowest price of the year, and if you didn't have a pass this season, you can still grab one and start skiing the rest of this winter. Either way, now's the time to buy and get the biggest bang for your buck.

We'll see you out there!

-Team Snow
